projects.name: CORNET2.0
projects.readme:
# CORNET2.0

Co-Simulation Framework for applications involving Networked Robots like Connected Autonomous Vehicles, Network of UAVs. 

---
 
CORNET 1.0 integrates Ardupilot SITL in Gazebo and NS3 and the source code is available [here](https://github.com/srikrishna3118/CORNET.git).

CORNET 2.0 is more generic framework that can be integrated with any robotic framework that supports ROS. This code was tested on an Ubuntu 16.04 and Ubuntu 18.04 system.

For network realization, we use Mininet Wi-Fi; the dependent packages need to installed as mentioned in mininet wifi documentation. This work is submitted for review (COMSNETS 2022). If you find this code useful in your research, please consider citing:

```
@misc{acharya2021cornet,
    title={CORNET 2.0: A Co-Simulation Middleware for Robot Networks}, 
    author={Srikrishna Acharya and Bharadwaj Amrutur and Mukunda Bharatheesha and Yogesh Simmhan},
    year={2021},
    eprint={2109.06979},
    archivePrefix={arXiv},
    primaryClass={cs.RO}
}
```

## Installation

### mininet-WiFi + Containernet Support

**We highly recommend using Ubuntu version 16.04 or higher. Some new hostapd features might not work on Ubuntu 14.04.**

```bash
sudo apt install libnl-route-3-dev
pip3 install docker-py
sudo update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/python python /usr/bin/python3 1 

sudo apt-get install git
sudo apt-get install ansible git aptitude
git clone https://github.com/ramonfontes/containernet.git
cd containernet/ansible
~/containernet/ansible$ sudo ansible-playbook -i "localhost," -c local install.yml
cd ..
~/containernet$ sudo python setup.py install
```

### ROS2

The Docker with ROS2 support provided in docker_container folder along with other wireless tools for testing and evaluation.

build.sh script will build and tag the images required for running. you can add you own Dockerfile based on the file Dockerfile

### Prebuilt docker image

You can also use a pre-built docker image which can be downloaded from [here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1PNWrrP4M3ikoyO_3HJ730YSZ5dLxTIO7/view?usp=sharing)
then you can load the image with the following command:

```bash
docker load -i containernet_foxy.tar
```

projects.name: CORNET
projects.readme:
# CORNET — Generic Co-Simulation Framework for Networked Robots

[![CI](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/CORNET/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/CORNET/actions)

**CORNET** is a task-driven, plugin-based co-simulation framework that lets you run network-robotics experiments with a single config file and one command:

```bash
python -m cornet tasks/pendulum_nr_control
```

## Features

- **Unified config schema** — one YAML covers network (NS-3 5G NR or Mininet-WiFi) + robot (Gazebo + ROS 2)
- **Plugin architecture** — swap network or robot backends without touching orchestration code
- **Task-folder convention** — each experiment is fully self-contained under `tasks/<name>/`
- **Parameter sweep** — declare axes in config; framework runs all combinations automatically
- **EvalTool interface** — standardized metric extraction per task
- **Experiment leaderboard** — append-only JSON + `rich` terminal viewer across runs

## Quickstart

```bash
pip install cornet-framework
# System prerequisites: NS-3 (with 5G NR), Mininet-WiFi, Gazebo Classic 11, ROS 2 Humble
python -m cornet tasks/pendulum_nr_control
python -m cornet view tasks/pendulum_nr_control
```

See [docs/INSTALL.md](docs/INSTALL.md) for system prerequisites and [docs/GETTING_STARTED.md](docs/GETTING_STARTED.md) for a walkthrough.

## Development

CORNET changes are tracked with OpenSpec (`/opsx:propose → /opsx:discuss → /opsx:apply → /opsx:archive`). The optional discuss phase (`/opsx:discuss`) runs adversarial critique and generates a `discussion.md` with binding implementation constraints before apply. See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#openspec-workflow).

## CORNET Family

| Version | Repo | Publication |
|---------|------|-------------|
| 1.0 | [srikrishna3118/CORNET](https://github.com/srikrishna3118/CORNET) | COMSNETS 2020 |
| 2.0 | [rbccps-iisc/CORNET2.0](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/CORNET2.0) | arXiv:2109.06979, COMSNETS 2022 |
| 3.0 | [rbccps-iisc/CORNET3.0](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/CORNET3.0) | PhD thesis (IISc, 2025) |
| Flagship | **this repo** | — |

See [docs/LINEAGE.md](docs/LINEAGE.md) for the full family history and BibTeX citations.

## License

MIT — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).

projects.name: modern-systems-engineering
projects.description: A practical curriculum for learning systems engineering using real-world engineering blogs and hands-on projects.
projects.readme:
# Modern Systems Engineering Curriculum

A practical, **production-focused** curriculum for learning systems engineering through real-world case studies and hands-on labs. This repository contains engineering lessons from 20+ leading tech companies building systems at scale.

## What You'll Learn

* **Distributed Systems**: Consensus, replication, fault tolerance, consistency models
* **Scale**: Horizontal and vertical scaling, sharding, caching, CDNs
* **Reliability**: High availability, disaster recovery, chaos engineering
* **Real-Time**: WebSockets, streaming, event-driven architectures
* **ML Systems**: Feature stores, serving infrastructure, model deployment
* **Financial Systems**: Idempotency, double-entry ledgers, compliance
* **Design Trade-offs**: Consistency vs. availability, latency vs. throughput

## Featured Companies

Netflix, Uber, Airbnb, AWS, Kafka, Google Spanner, YouTube, WhatsApp, Cloudflare, Pinterest, Slack, Spotify, NVIDIA, Shopify, GitHub, Discord, Stripe, Figma, and more.

**The goal is to help engineers understand how production systems actually work — not just theory, but real engineering decisions, trade-offs, and lessons learned at scale.**

---

## 🚀 Start Here

**New to this curriculum?** Read [Getting Started](GETTING_STARTED.md) for a quick-start guide and recommended learning paths.

---

## Course Roadmap

| Course | Topic | Duration | Labs | Key Topics |
|--------|-------|----------|------|-----------|
| **01** | [Engineering Foundations](course-01-engineering-foundations/syllabus.md) | 4 weeks | 2 | SOLID, APIs, reliability, version control |
| **02** | [Distributed Systems](course-02-distributed-systems/syllabus.md) | 5 weeks | 2 ([Chat](course-02-distributed-systems/labs/chat-system), [Kafka](course-02-distributed-systems/labs/kafka-pipeline)) | Consensus, replication, consistency, CAP |
| **03** | [Cloud Infrastructure](course-03-cloud-infrastructure/syllabus.md) | 4 weeks | 1 ([Kubernetes](course-03-cloud-infrastructure/labs/kubernetes-deployment)) | Containers, orchestration, multi-region |
| **04** | [AI Systems](course-04-ai-systems/syllabus.md) | 4 weeks | 1 ([RAG](course-04-ai-systems/labs/rag-ai-assistant)) | Inference, embeddings, vector DBs, serving |
| **05** | [Real-Time Systems](course-05-realtime-systems/syllabus.md) | 3 weeks | 1 ([WebSocket](course-05-realtime-systems/labs/websocket-streaming)) | Streaming, pub/sub, collaboration |
| **06** | [Financial Systems](course-06-financial-systems/syllabus.md) | 3 weeks | 1 ([Ledger](course-06-financial-systems/labs/payment-ledger)) | Idempotency, double-entry, compliance |
| **07** | [Advanced Design](course-07-advanced-system-design/syllabus.md) | 4 weeks | [5 Exercises](course-07-advanced-system-design/exercises/system-design-exercises.md) | Design trade-offs, interview prep |
| **Capstone** | [Project Guide](capstone-project/project-guide.md) | 4–6 weeks | [4 Templates](capstone-project/templates/) | Full-stack system design |

---

## Quick Navigation

**🎓 Lectures** (where available)
* [Engineering Fundamentals](course-01-engineering-foundations/lectures/lecture-01-engineering-fundamentals.md)
* [Building Scalable Systems](course-01-engineering-foundations/lectures/lecture-02-building-scalable-systems.md)
* [Distributed Systems Fundamentals](course-02-distributed-systems/lectures/lecture-01-distributed-fundamentals.md)

**💼 Case Studies** (Learn from 20+ production systems)
* [All Case Studies →](case-studies/)
* **Messaging Systems**: [WhatsApp](case-studies/whatsapp-messaging-system.md), [Slack](case-studies/slack-message-queue.md), [Discord](case-studies/discord-chat-infrastructure.md)
* **Search & Discovery**: [Uber](case-studies/uber-dispatch-system.md), [Airbnb](case-studies/airbnb-search-platform.md), [Pinterest](case-studies/pinterest-feed-ranking.md), [GitHub](case-studies/github-code-search.md)
* **Streaming & Media**: [Netflix](case-studies/netflix-streaming-architecture.md), [YouTube](case-studies/youtube-video-delivery.md), [Figma](case-studies/figma-realtime-collaboration.md)
* **Data & Infrastructure**: [Kafka](case-studies/kafka-event-streaming.md), [Google Spanner](case-studies/google-spanner-consistency.md), [AWS Cells](case-studies/aws-cell-architecture.md)
* **ML & Recommendations**: [Spotify](case-studies/spotify-recommendation-pipeline.md), [NVIDIA GPU](case-studies/nvidia-ai-cluster-architecture.md), [OpenAI Inference](case-studies/openai-inference-infrastructure.md)
* **Payments & Scale**: [Stripe](case-studies/stripe-payment-processing.md), [Shopify Flash Sales](case-studies/shopify-flash-sale-scaling.md), [Uber Microservices](case-studies/uber-microservices-migration.md)

**🛠️ Labs** (8 hands-on projects with starter code & solutions)
* [API Task Service](course-01-engineering-foundations/labs/api-task-service) → API contracts, validation, pagination
* [Reliability Toolkit](course-01-engineering-foundations/labs/reliability-toolkit) → Retries, backoff, circuit breaker
* [Chat System (gRPC)](course-02-distributed-systems/labs/chat-system) → Bidirectional streaming, chat rooms
* [Kafka Pipeline](course-02-distributed-systems/labs/kafka-pipeline) → Event ingestion, analytics aggregation
* [Kubernetes Deployment](course-03-cloud-infrastructure/labs/kubernetes-deployment) → Health checks, scaling, resource limits
* [WebSocket Streaming](course-05-realtime-systems/labs/websocket-streaming) → Real-time pub/sub, backpressure handling
* [RAG AI Assistant](course-04-ai-systems/labs/rag-ai-assistant) → Embeddings, semantic search, LLM generation
* [Payment Ledger](course-06-financial-systems/labs/payment-ledger) → Double-entry bookkeeping, idempotency

**🏗️ Architecture Reference**
* [Architecture Diagrams Guide](architecture-diagrams-guide.md) — 12 patterns: scaling, caching, replication, partitioning, microservices, event-driven, global distribution, search, streaming, security, monitoring

**📋 System Design Exercises** (Interview-style problems)
* [5 Advanced Exercises](course-07-advanced-system-design/exercises/system-design-exercises.md)
  * Design a URL Shortener
  * Real-Time Notification System
  * Rate Limiter (Token Bucket, Sliding Window)
  * Full-Text Search Engine
  * Video Streaming Platform

**🚀 Capstone Projects** (4 complete project templates)
* [AI Knowledge Platform](capstone-project/templates/ai-knowledge-platform.md) — RAG system with embeddings and retrieval
* [Global Chat Platform](capstone-project/templates/global-chat-platform.md) — Multi-region WebSockets with consistency
* [Payment Processing](capstone-project/templates/payment-processing-platform.md) — Double-entry ledger with idempotency
* [Feed Ranking System](capstone-project/templates/feed-ranking-system.md) — ML-based personalization with two-stage ranking
* [Complete Guide](capstone-project/project-guide.md) — Starting, scoping, and completing your capstone

---

## 📚 Reference & Support

**📖 [Glossary](GLOSSARY.md)** — 100+ key terms and concepts used throughout the curriculum (distributed systems, scaling, databases, ML systems, etc.)

**🔗 [Resources & References](RESOURCES.md)** — Curated books, blogs, papers, tools, communities, and newsletters
* Recommended books (Kleppmann, Nygard, Newman, Google SRE)
* Company engineering blogs (Netflix, Uber, Stripe, Discord, etc.)
* Essential papers (Google File System, Spanner, Cassandra, Raft)
* Tools and technologies (Docker, Kubernetes, Kafka, databases)
* Staying current (newsletters, podcasts, influencers)

## Repository Structure

```
modern-systems-engineering/
├── README.md
├── curriculum-roadmap.md
├── architecture-diagrams-guide.md         # 12 architecture patterns
│
├── case-studies/                          # 20+ production systems
│   ├── netflix-streaming-architecture.md
│   ├── uber-dispatch-system.md
│   ├── kafka-event-streaming.md
│   └── ... (15 more case studies)
│
├── course-01-engineering-foundations/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── lectures/
│   │   ├── lecture-01-engineering-fundamentals.md
│   │   └── lecture-02-building-scalable-systems.md
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-02-distributed-systems/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── lectures/
│   │   └── lecture-01-distributed-fundamentals.md
│   ├── labs/
│   │   ├── chat-system/          (gRPC, bidirectional streaming)
│   │   └── kafka-pipeline/       (events, analytics aggregation)
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-03-cloud-infrastructure/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── labs/
│   │   └── kubernetes-deployment/  (Docker, K8s manifests, health probes)
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-04-ai-systems/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── labs/
│   │   └── rag-ai-assistant/    (embeddings, vector DB, LLM)
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-05-realtime-systems/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── labs/
│   │   └── websocket-streaming/  (pub/sub, backpressure)
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-06-financial-systems/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── labs/
│   │   └── payment-ledger/      (double-entry, idempotency)
│   └── case-studies/
│
├── course-07-advanced-system-design/
│   ├── syllabus.md
│   ├── exercises/
│   │   └── system-design-exercises.md  (URL shortener, rate limiter, search, etc.)
│   └── case-studies/
│
└── capstone-project/
    ├── project-guide.md
  └── templates/
    ├── ai-knowledge-platform.md
    ├── global-chat-platform.md
    ├── payment-processing-platform.md
    └── feed-ranking-system.md
```

---

---

## Learning Outcomes

After completing this curriculum, you will be able to:

✅ **Design scalable systems** — horizontal scaling, sharding, caching, load balancing
✅ **Build distributed systems** — consensus algorithms, replication, consistency models, fault tolerance
✅ **Deploy cloud infrastructure** — containerization, orchestration, multi-region deployment
✅ **Handle real-time data** — streaming, pub/sub, WebSockets, event-driven architecture
✅ **Serve ML models** — embeddings, vector databases, inference optimization, serving infrastructure
✅ **Build financial systems** — idempotency, double-entry ledgers, compliance, auditability
✅ **Solve system design problems** — make trade-offs, estimate capacity, architect for failures
✅ **Learn from production systems** — understand how Netflix, Uber, Stripe, and others achieve massive scale

---

## How to Use This Repository

### 👥 For Self-Study
1. **Start**: Read [curriculum-roadmap.md](curriculum-roadmap.md) and pick a course
2. **Learn the theory**: Read the lecture notes (where available) and case studies for that course
3. **Get hands-on**: Complete the lab with starter code (try it yourself first!)
4. **Reference solution**: Compare your solution with the provided one
5. **Reinforce**: Study the case study architectures related to that course
6. **Advance**: Move to the next course

### 👨‍🏫 For Instructors
* Use **lectures** as base content for classroom instruction
* Use **case studies** as real-world examples during lectures
* Assign **labs** as homework or in-class exercises
* Reference **system design exercises** for assessment
* Guide capstone projects using provided templates

### 🎯 For Interviews
* Study [Advanced System Design Exercises](course-07-advanced-system-design/exercises/system-design-exercises.md) for interview prep
* Review [case studies](case-studies/) for architectural knowledge
* Reference [Architecture Diagrams Guide](architecture-diagrams-guide.md) to explain trade-offs
* Practice back-of-envelope calculations from labs

### 🚀 For Building Real Products
* Start with **case studies** related to your domain
* Implement a **lab** in your tech stack to understand patterns
* Use **capstone templates** as starting points for your architecture
* Reference **architecture diagrams** when designing systems

---

## Key Features

* **20+ Production Case Studies** — Real systems from Netflix, Uber, Stripe, GitHub, Discord, etc.
* **8 Hands-On Labs** — Progressive difficulty with starter code and complete solutions
* **3 Lecture Series** — Foundations, scalability, and distributed systems theory
* **System Design Exercises** — Interview-style problems with detailed solutions
* **4 Capstone Templates** — Complete architectures you can build
* **Architecture Reference Guide** — 12 common patterns with ASCII diagrams
* **Code Examples** — Python, Go, SQL, YAML for cloud-native development

---

## Technology Stack Overview

**Languages**: Python, Go, Node.js, Java
**Databases**: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Cassandra, DynamoDB, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch
**Message Queues**: Kafka, RabbitMQ, Redis Streams  
**Infrastructure**: Kubernetes, Docker, AWS, GCP, Azure
**ML/AI**: Embeddings, Vector Databases (Pinecone/Weaviate), LLMs
**Protocols**: HTTP/REST, gRPC, WebSockets

---

## What's New (Recent Additions)

✨ **20+ Case Studies** — Real production architectures from leading companies
✨ **Labs** — 8 hands-on labs with starter code + reference solutions (where available)
✨ **Lecture Notes** — Detailed lecture sets covering fundamentals, scalability, and distributed systems
✨ **System Design Exercises** — 5 interview-style problems with comprehensive solutions
✨ **4 Capstone Templates** — Production-ready architecture templates across 4 domains
✨ **Architecture Diagrams Guide** — 12 patterns with ASCII diagrams for quick reference

projects.name: 5g_opt_drl
projects.readme:
# 5g_opt_drl

### Installation
1. Go to the network folder and run the initial script which downloads ns-3.33, configures and builds. This script needs to be executed only once and it may take a while to finish.
```
$ cd network
$ ./net_init.sh
```

projects.name: nvidia_auvidea_j120support
projects.readme:
# nvidia_avuidea_j120support
The guide is generated by following steps in the link given below 

https://developer.ridgerun.com/wiki/index.php?title=Compiling_Jetson_TX2_source_code_L4T_32.1
#### Follow these instructions to build and install the kernel Image and device tree.
### 1. Download and install the Toolchain
###### NVIDIA recommends using the Linaro 7.3.1 2018.05 toolchain for L4T 32.*

###### Execute the following commands to extract the toolchain:

`mkdir $HOME/l4t-gcc`

`cd $HOME/l4t-gcc`

`wget http://releases.linaro.org/components/toolchain/binaries/7.3-2018.05/aarch64-linux-gnu/gcc-linaro-7.3.1-2018.05-x86_64_aarch64-linux-gnu.tar.xz
tar xf gcc-linaro-7.3.1-2018.05-x86_64_aarch64-linux-gnu.tar.xz`

### 2. Download the kernel sources
###### You can download the kernel source files and then manually extract them. it is recommended to instead sync with git.
###### In a browser, navigate to: https://developer.nvidia.com/embedded/downloads. Locate and download the L4T Sources for your release. (L4T Sources 32.*) and extract the files in the same directory

###### Execute the following commands to extract the kernel:

`cd public_sources/`

`JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE=$(pwd)`

`tar -xvf kernel_src.tbz2`

###### Before step 3, you need to copy the attached .dts and .dtsi files into the respective files in the kernel directory
`$HOME/l4t-gcc/public_sources//hardware/nvidia/platform/t18x/` 

`../t18x/common`

`../t18x/quill`

### 3. Compile kernel and dtb

###### Follow the steps:
`cd $JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE`

`CROSS_COMPILE=$HOME/l4t-gcc/gcc-linaro-7.3.1-2018.05-x86_64_aarch64-linux-gnu/bin/aarch64-linux-gnu-`

`KERNEL_OUT=$JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE/build`
`KERNEL_MODULES_OUT=$JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE/modules`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT tegra_defconfig`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT menuconfig # if is required to change the default configuration`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT CROSS_COMPILE=${CROSS_COMPILE} -j6 Image`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT CROSS_COMPILE=${CROSS_COMPILE} -j6 dtbs`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT CROSS_COMPILE=${CROSS_COMPILE} -j6 modules`

`make -C kernel/kernel-4.9/ ARCH=arm64 O=$KERNEL_OUT modules_install INSTALL_MOD_PATH=$KERNEL_MODULES_OUT`

### Flash Jetson TX2
###### This guide assumes that the user already have JetPack 4.2 installed. This link contains details about how to install JetPack 4.2: https://docs.nvidia.com/sdk-manager/download-run-sdkm/index.html

###### Make sure the Jetson TX2 is in recovery mode

###### JETPACK_4_2 contains the directory where JetPack 4.2 was installed. For example.


`cd ${JETPACK_4_2}/JetPack_4.2_Linux_P3310/Linux_for_Tegra`
###### Copy kernel generated
`cp $JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE/build/arch/arm64/boot/Image kernel/`
###### Copy device tree generated
`cp $JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE/build/arch/arm64/boot/dts/tegra186-quill-p3310-1000-c03-00-base.dtb kernel/dtb/`
###### Copy new modules
`sudo cp -a $JETSON_TX2_KERNEL_SOURCE/modules/lib rootfs/`
###### Flash memory 
`sudo ./flash.sh jetson-tx2 mmcblk0p1`

projects.name: AutonomousDrones
projects.description: This repository will contain code and parameters for the Autonomous Drones project.
projects.readme:
# AutonomousDrones  
This is a project to establish and execute a successful surveillance algorithm using multiple drones.  

## System and Software Versions -  
	OS: Ubuntu 18.04  
  	Gazebo: gazebo-9  
  	ROS: ROS melodic  
	GCS: QGroundControl  
  	Python: python3.6.9 
	
## Installations -  

1. [Ubuntu](https://itsfoss.com/install-ubuntu-1404-dual-boot-mode-windows-8-81-uefi/)    

2. [Gazebo](http://gazebosim.org/tutorials?tut=install_ubuntu)  
	Please note that installation link might be updated to a later version of Gazebo!  
	
3. [ROS](https://github.com/Intelligent-Quads/iq_tutorials/blob/master/docs/installing_ros.md)

4. [ArduPilot](https://github.com/Suchit153/ardupilot)  
	Recommended: Fork and clone the above repo!  	

5. [QGroundControl](https://docs.qgroundcontrol.com/master/en/getting_started/download_and_install.html)  
	Please note that we only require the installation for Ubuntu Linux!  

## Additional Setup -  
In order to support a multi-drone framework, we need to make some additional changes -  

1. setup qgroundcontrol (QGC) to accept multiple vehicles -  
	* Open QGC
	* navigate to the settings tab and click on Comm Links  
	* Click Add  
	* Fill in the vehicle TCP port as [shown](https://github.com/Intelligent-Quads/iq_tutorials/blob/master/docs/imgs/qg_comms.png)  
	* Repeat for adding new vehicles on different TCP ports  
	* __Note:__ TCP ports for each vehicle are separated by a value of 100. Therefore, vehicle1 = 8100, vehicle2 = 8200, vehicle3 = 8300 ...
	
## Simulation Prerequisite -

* Make a catkin workspace in your home directory -  
  > $ mkdir -p ~/catkin_proj/src  
  > $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src  
  > $ git clone https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones.git  
  > $ cd ~/catkin_proj  
  > $ catkin build --cmake-args -DPYTHON_VERSION=3.6  
	
## Simulation Setup -

* Models
  * The models used in the environment are located in [models](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones/tree/master/Simulation/models)  

* Worlds
  * The world files used for using these models in gazebo is located in [worlds](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones/tree/master/Simulation/worlds)
  
* Launch Files
  * The launch files used for using these models in gazebo is located in [launch files](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones/tree/master/launch)
  
* Scripts
  * The scripts for running simulations are located in [scripts](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones/tree/master/Simulation/scripts)  
  * The scripts for running on the drone's on-board computer are located in [scripts](https://github.com/rbccps-iisc/AutonomousDrones/tree/master/Drone/scripts)  
  * Description of the functionality of each script in this directory is mentioned as a comment at the beginning of the script file.  
 
## Simulation Usage - 
Run each of the following steps in separate terminals - 

1. launch gazebo world:  
	> $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src/AutonomousDrones  
	> $ LaunchAPM.sh  
	
2. start ardupilot for multiple drones:  
	> $ cd ~/ardupilot/Tools/autotest  
	> $ ./multi-ardupilot.sh  
	
3. launch mavros for multiple drones:  
	> $ roslaunch iq_sim multi-am.launch  
	
4. start QGC:  
	> $ ./QGroundControl.AppImage  
	* In QGC, navigate to the settings tab and click on Comm Links. Then, connect all three vehicles.  
	
5. Running Scripts:  
	* Terminal 1  
		> $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src/AutonomousDrones/Simulation/scripts   
		> $ python3 drone1_mission.py  
	* Terminal 2  
		> $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src/AutonomousDrones/Simulation/scripts   
		> $ python3 drone2_mission.py  
	* Terminal 3  
		> $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src/AutonomousDrones/Simulation/scripts   
		> $ python3 drone3_mission.py  
	* Terminal 4  
		> $ cd ~/catkin_proj/src/AutonomousDrones/Simulation/scripts   
		> $ python3 gcs_control.py

projects.name: ChoiRbot
projects.readme: # ChoiRbot [![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/choirbot/badge/?version=latest)](https://choirbot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest) [**Website**](https://opt4smart.github.io/ChoiRbot/) | [**Reference docs**](https://choirbot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) | [**Installation**](#requirements-and-installation) | [**Getting started**](#getting-started) | :warning: Information for end users | |:------------------------------------| | Documentation pages are currently being uploaded. The latest version will be available soon. | **ChoiRbot** is a ROS 2 toolbox developed within the excellence research program ERC in the project [OPT4SMART](http://opt4smart.dei.unibo.it). **ChoiRbot** provides a comprehensive set of libraries to execute complex distributed multi-robot tasks, such as model predictive control and task assignment, either in simulation or experimentally. The toolbox focuses on networks of heterogeneous robots without a central coordinator. It provides utilities for the solution of distributed optimization problems and can be also used to implement distributed feedback laws. Specifically, the package allows you to - Encode distributed optimization and control algorithms - Perform peer-to-peer communications among robots - Develop planning and control schemes - Connect with external motion capture hardware (see also our [ROS 2 Vicon Bridge](https://github.com/OPT4SMART/ros2-vicon-receiver)) - Run experiments on your robotic fleet - Perform realistic simulations with [Gazebo](http://gazebosim.org) and visualize data with [RVIZ](https://github.com/ros2/rviz) ## Requirements and Installation **ChoiRbot** requires ROS 2 Dashing Diademata to be installed on your system. It relies on * numpy * scipy * recordclass * dill * disropt (optional, but required for several features) Please, refer to the [installation page](https://opt4smart.github.io/ChoiRbot/installation) for a more detailed installation guide. To install the toolbox, first source your ROS 2 installation. Then create a ROS 2 workspace and, inside the `src` directory, run: ``` git clone https://github.com/OPT4SMART/ChoiRbot.git . ``` Then, from the parent directory execute: ``` colcon build --symlink-install ``` ## Getting started * [Quick start](https://choirbot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quick_start/index.html) * [Examples](https://choirbot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/examples/index.html) ## Citing **ChoiRbot** If you are you using **ChoiRbot** in research work to be published, please cite the accompanying paper ``` @article{testa2021choirbot, title={ChoiRbot: A ROS 2 toolbox for cooperative robotics}, author={Testa, Andrea and Camisa, Andrea and Notarstefano, Giuseppe}, journal={IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters}, volume={6}, number={2}, pages={2714--2720}, year={2021}, publisher={IEEE} } ``` ## Contributors **ChoiRbot** is developed by [Andrea Testa](https://andrea-testa.github.io), [Andrea Camisa](https://www.unibo.it/sitoweb/a.camisa) and [Giuseppe Notarstefano](https://www.unibo.it/sitoweb/giuseppe.notarstefano) ## Acknowledgements This result is part of a project that has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement No 638992 - OPT4SMART).
